topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Silvassa
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Silvassa or locally known as JNV Silvassa is a boarding, co-educational school in Union territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

topPlaces.name: Amli, India
topPlaces.description: Amli is a census town in the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu in India.

topPlaces.name: Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli
topPlaces.description: Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli was a de facto independent political entity that existed on the Indian subcontinent between 1954 and 1961. It was declared by pro-India forces that had gained control of the region from Portugal in 1954, and ceased to exist after being formally annexed by India on 11 August 1961 as the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli.

topPlaces.name: Silvassa
topPlaces.description: Silvassa is a city and the headquarters of the Dadra and Nagar Haveli district in the Union territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu in western India. It is today the largest city in the union territory.

Amli is a census town in the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu in India.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli & around

items.note: Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli was a de facto independent political entity that existed on the Indian subcontinent between 1954 and 1961. It was declared by pro-India forces that had gained control of the region from Portugal in 1954, and ceased to exist after being formally annexed by India on 11 August 1961 as the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli.

Silvassa is a city and the headquarters of the Dadra and Nagar Haveli district in the Union territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu in western India. It is today the largest city in the union territory.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Surat Airport (99 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dadra and Nagar Haveli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Silvassa, Amli, India, Free Dadra and Nagar Haveli, Silvassa.
